mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over the medium term.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS shows no significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day returns over any 5-year rolling window, or if returns are negative despite strong RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes a broader slowdown in economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A very low federal funds rate combined with strong housing starts indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a robust housing market, which boosts broader economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat when FEDFUNDS is below 0.2% and HOUST is above 1500k units in more than 40% of occurrences over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth during periods of low inflation suggests healthy, non-inflationary economic expansion, which is positive for corporate profitability and thus S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are consistently negative when INDPRO growth is strong and CPIAUCSL annual change is below 2%, or if the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in real GDP growth indicates a weakening economy, which pressures corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a negative GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strong and expanding labor market, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over the longer term.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate shows no significant positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day returns over any 7-year rolling window, or if returns are negative despite strong PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low unemployment suggests an overheating economy, which often prompts central bank tightening, negatively impacting equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are consistently positive when CPIAUCSL annual change is above 4% and UNRATE is below 4%, or if the average return is not significantly negative in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts during periods of low interest rates indicate robust investment and consumer confidence, which fuels economic growth and supports equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat when HOUST is strong and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, in more than 35% of triggered instances over a 10-year period.
